# Transcrição: Staccato_vs_Legato

- **Fonte:** Staccato_vs_Legato
- **Duração:** 01:27
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:27]

In music there's a concept called staccato and legato. Let's say I'm a pianist and I was playing the song twinkle twinkle little star. Notice the difference is that with staccato you can hear the notes. However with legato you don't only just hear it you actually can feel it. All the notes are connected together so it creates more of an emotional experience for people. It moves people more whereas staccato is kind of choppy. This is so important because it applies to your voice and when you speak. Imagine I was doing the entire video that you watched and I got stuck in staccato mode. Notice how again you heard the words but again it wasn't as easy to listen to it wasn't as engaging. While staccato is not a good way of speaking all the time it's important to use staccato when you have something powerful to say. However if you don't have anything that important to say then make sure you engage in legato. This small little difference in you adjusting the way you speak and ensuring that you aren't default in staccato but that your default in legato will have a huge impact on the way you communicate and also it makes you more engaging to listen to it, it makes you more pleasant to listen to.
